facts: The system register incoming and outgoing flights with their estimated times.
The system allocate each flight the earliest available 5-minute slot on either runway at or after its estimated time, with a 3-minute separation gap between consecutive slots on the same runway.
The system give arrivals priority over departures when competing for the same slot.
The system detect and prevent any overlapping or gap-violating assignments on the same runway.

The system allow a runway to be closed, automatically reassigning its future flights to the other runway and marking flights as delayed if they cannot be placed within 60 minutes of their estimated time.
The system immediately give a declared emergency flight the earliest possible slot and re-queue displaced flights by the normal rules.
The system show a slot timetable per runway with delayed flights and emergency events.

evidence.claim: The system enables users to register flights with their estimated times.
evidence.fact: The system register incoming and outgoing flights with their estimated times.

evidence.claim: The system enables users to close a runway and have the system automatically reassign flights to the other runway, marking delayed flights clearly.
evidence.fact: The system allow a runway to be closed, automatically reassigning its future flights to the other runway and marking flights as delayed if they cannot be placed within 60 minutes of their estimated time.

evidence.claim: The system enables users to declare an emergency and have the system immediately assign the earliest slot, displacing other flights as needed.
evidence.fact: The system immediately give a declared emergency flight the earliest possible slot and re-queue displaced flights by the normal rules.

evidence.claim: The system enables users to view a clear timetable per runway showing all slots, delayed flights, and emergency events.
evidence.fact: The system show a slot timetable per runway with delayed flights and emergency events.
